query.task_summary: Use browser-use on my logged-in Chrome to monitor Twitter AI news.

priors.summary: Portable Example Laptop hosts often lack gui-agent and external-storage-backed qwen_local capacity. Prefer shell, Chrome, Claude Code, or remote-heavy paths unless live runtime facts confirm gui-agent or qwen_local are available.

browser-use runs a controlled browser session and should not be assumed to hijack an already logged-in personal Chrome window. If the task depends on the user's live personal session, use direct desktop/browser control instead.

Twitter/X monitoring should start from trusted Following sources and first-party event signals, not keyword search. Search is for targeted follow-up only, to avoid context pollution from low-trust posts.

vertex_local may be configured in AGN but unavailable on a given host unless the local proxy endpoint is live. Treat live host state as the final authority before planning around Vertex local execution.

fact_payload.intent: keep runtime facts ahead of memory

runtime_priority_notice: Runtime facts remain authoritative. Memory priors are advisory and may be confirmed or overridden by live host state.
